items.headline: U.S. Markets Face Continued Headwinds; Futures Signal Cautious Open
items.summary: U.S. stock markets closed lower on Thursday, marking their lowest closes in months for the Dow and S&P 500, as concerns over Middle East geopolitical tensions and persistent inflationary pressures weighed heavily on investor sentiment. All three major indices are on track for their fourth consecutive weekly decline. Pre-market trading for Friday indicates a cautious start, with futures for major indices showing slight declines after an earlier wobble despite easing fears regarding the U.S.-Iran conflict.

items.statContext: Down 0.3% (-18.21 pts). Lowest close in four months, below its 200-day moving average.
Slid 0.3% (-61.73 pts). Closed below its 200-day moving average.
Fell 0.4% (-203.72 pts). Marked the lowest close for 2026, below its 200-day moving average.

items.headline: Micron Technology Inc. Tumbles on Disappointing Guidance
items.summary: The AI-powered semiconductor giant saw its stock tumble 3.9% as its third-quarter fiscal 2026 guidance failed to impress the market.

items.headline: U Power Limited Plummets on Dilutive Public Offering
items.summary: Shares of U Power Limited plunged 65.08% after the company announced and priced a $6 million public offering of units at a significant discount, triggering panic selling due to heavy dilution.

items.headline: Reviva Pharmaceuticals Holdings Down on Discounted Offering
items.summary: Reviva Pharmaceuticals saw a 55.43% decline following the pricing of a $10 million public offering of shares plus warrants at a discount to its prior close, a common trigger for selling in biotech stocks.

items.headline: Tilly's Inc. Leads March Monthly Gainers
items.summary: Tilly's Inc. has been a standout performer for the month of March, showing a significant gain of +158.7%.

items.headline: SOC Continues Strong Monthly Performance
items.summary: SOC has seen robust performance in March, climbing +96.0% month-to-date.

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions Continue to Drive Oil Volatility
items.summary: The ongoing Middle East conflict between the U.S.-Israel and Iran remains a primary driver of market uncertainty, particularly impacting crude oil and natural gas prices and fueling inflationary concerns. While comments from Israel's Prime Minister briefly eased worries, oil prices continued to show volatility.

items.headline: Fed Holds Rates Steady Amid Inflationary Concerns
items.summary: The Federal Reserve maintained its federal funds rate range of 3.5-3.75% at its March FOMC meeting. Chairman Jerome Powell emphasized the need for clearer progress on inflation before considering rate cuts, with the latest dot-plot indicating only one 25-basis-point cut in 2026.

items.headline: Defensive and 'Real Economy' Sectors Outperforming Tech in Ongoing Rotation
items.summary: An observable market rotation continues in 2026, with industrial, consumer defensive, and energy sectors leading gains as technology stocks face challenges. Investors are diversifying beyond AI-focused names towards 'real economy' stocks amid broader market uncertainty. On Thursday, all 11 S&P 500 sectors closed in negative territory, with Consumer Discretionary, Health Care, and Consumer Staples among the biggest decliners.

items.headline: Mixed Economic Data Presents Unclear Picture for Economy
items.summary: Recent economic data includes a decrease in initial jobless claims for the week ended March 14 to 205,000, below consensus estimates. However, new home sales in January missed expectations, while the Philadelphia Fed Index for March reached its highest level for 2026.
